title: astragalus lentiginosus var. palans
intro: Astragalus lentiginosus var. palans, also known as the lesser Utah milkvetch, is a plant species in the legume family that grows in the western United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and immunomodulatory effects. The lesser Utah milkvetch is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, boosting the immune system, and potentially lowering blood pressure, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Astragalus lentiginosus var. palans is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, capsules, and tinctures, although the quality and safety of these preparations can vary.
